model_narrative: We run a paper mill with several machines that can each produce several grades of paper. For every grade we know its monthly demand, its selling price, and on each machine its production rate and its production cost per ton. We decide how many tons of each grade to produce on each machine. The objective is to make the total monthly profit as large as possible.

sets.name: g
sets.doc: the grades of paper that can be produced

params.name: prate
params.doc: production rate of a grade on a machine, in tons per hour

params.name: pcost
params.doc: production cost of a grade on a machine, in dollars per ton

params.name: dempr_demand
params.doc: monthly demand for each grade that must be produced exactly, in tons per month

params.name: dempr_price
params.doc: selling price of each grade, in dollars per ton

params.name: avail
params.doc: available machine time on each machine, in hours per month

vars.name: outp
vars.doc: the tons of a grade produced on a machine per month

vars.name: profit
vars.doc: total monthly profit in dollars per month

description: Each machine has only so many hours of running time available each month. The time a machine spends on a grade is the tons of that grade produced on it divided by how fast that grade runs on that machine. For each machine, the total running time it spends across all grades must not exceed the time available on that machine.

model_narrative: We run a paper mill with several machines that can each produce several grades of paper. For every grade we know its monthly demand, its selling price, and on each machine its production rate and its production cost per ton. We decide how many tons of each grade to produce on each machine. The objective is to make the total monthly profit as large as possible.

sets.name: g
sets.doc: the grades of paper that can be produced

params.name: prate
params.doc: production rate of a grade on a machine, in tons per hour

params.name: pcost
params.doc: production cost of a grade on a machine, in dollars per ton

params.name: dempr_demand
params.doc: monthly demand for each grade that must be produced exactly, in tons per month

params.name: dempr_price
params.doc: selling price of each grade, in dollars per ton

params.name: avail
params.doc: available machine time on each machine, in hours per month

vars.name: outp
vars.doc: the tons of a grade produced on a machine per month

vars.name: profit
vars.doc: total monthly profit in dollars per month

description: Total profit is the revenue from selling every grade at its demand quantity times its price, minus the cost of producing each grade on each machine, summed over all grades and machines. Set the profit variable equal to that net amount.
expected_pyomo:
def profit_def_rule(model):
    return model.profit == sum(model.dempr_demand[g] * model.dempr_price[g] for g in model.g) \
           - sum(model.pcost[g, m] * model.outp[g, m] for g in model.g for m in model.m)
model.pdef = Constraint(rule=profit_def_rule)

model_narrative: We run a paper mill with several machines that can each produce several grades of paper. For every grade we know its monthly demand, its selling price, and on each machine its production rate and its production cost per ton. We decide how many tons of each grade to produce on each machine. The objective is to make the total monthly profit as large as possible.

sets.name: g
sets.doc: the grades of paper that can be produced

params.name: prate
params.doc: production rate of a grade on a machine, in tons per hour

params.name: pcost
params.doc: production cost of a grade on a machine, in dollars per ton

params.name: dempr_demand
params.doc: monthly demand for each grade that must be produced exactly, in tons per month

params.name: dempr_price
params.doc: selling price of each grade, in dollars per ton

params.name: avail
params.doc: available machine time on each machine, in hours per month

vars.name: outp
vars.doc: the tons of a grade produced on a machine per month

vars.name: profit
vars.doc: total monthly profit in dollars per month

description: To build the complete model, enforce the following relationships in order. First, each machine has only so many hours of running time available each month. The time a machine spends on a grade is the tons of that grade produced on it divided by how fast that grade runs on that machine. For each machine, the total running time it spends across all grades must not exceed the time available on that machine. Second, every grade has a fixed monthly demand that must be met exactly, no more and no less. For each grade, the total tons of it produced across all machines must equal its demand. Finally, total profit is the revenue from selling every grade at its demand quantity times its price, minus the cost of producing each grade on each machine, summed over all grades and machines. Set the profit variable equal to that net amount.
